item.title: LastPass Warns of New Phishing Campaign
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
The attackers are sending out fake alerts claiming unauthorized access or master password changes.

The post LastPass Warns of New Phishing Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Webinar Today: Designing an OT SOC for Safety, Reliability, and Business Continuity
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News
item.description:
Join the webinar as we explore a blueprint for an OT SOC leveraging an integrated OT Security Platform to safeguard operations and maintain business continuity.

The post Webinar Today: Designing an OT SOC for Safety, Reliability, and Business Continuity appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Google Plans Two-Week Release Schedule for Chrome
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
Starting September 2026, new Chrome iterations will be released twice as fast, part of a two-week cycle.

The post Google Plans Two-Week Release Schedule for Chrome appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Global Coalition Publishes 6G Security and Resilience Principles
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
The principles cover security, resilience against attacks and disasters, AI, and openness and interoperability.

The post Global Coalition Publishes 6G Security and Resilience Principles appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Critical FreeScout Vulnerability Leads to Full Server Compromise
item.dc:creator: Ionut Arghire
item.description:
A patch bypass for an authenticated code execution bug, the flaw leads to zero-click remote code execution attacks.

The post Critical FreeScout Vulnerability Leads to Full Server Compromise appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: VMware Aria Operations Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
The recently patched CVE-2026-22719 can be exploited by an unauthenticated attacker for remote code execution. 

The post VMware Aria Operations Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Iranian Strikes on Amazon Data Centers Highlight Industry’s Vulnerability to Physical Disasters
item.dc:creator: Associated Press
item.description:
Two AWS data centers in the United Arab Emirates were “directly struck” and another facility in Bahrain was also damaged after a drone landed nearby.

The post Iranian Strikes on Amazon Data Centers Highlight Industry’s Vulnerability to Physical Disasters appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Fig Security Launches With $38 Million to Bolster SecOps Resilience
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek News
item.description:
The company was founded in March 2025 and it has now emerged from stealth mode.

The post Fig Security Launches With $38 Million to Bolster SecOps Resilience appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Honeywell, Researcher Clash Over Impact of Building Controller Vulnerability
item.dc:creator: Eduard Kovacs
item.description:
The researcher says he has identified thousands of internet-exposed IQ4 building management controllers.

The post Honeywell, Researcher Clash Over Impact of Building Controller Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Quantum Decryption of RSA Is Much Closer Than Expected
item.dc:creator: Kevin Townsend
item.description:
For decades, the quantum threat to RSA and ECC encryption has been tied to Shor’s algorithm and the assumption that we would need million-qubit quantum computers to make it practical. A newly announced algorithm challenges that assumption and suggests the breaking point could arrive far sooner than expected.

The post Quantum Decryption of RSA Is Much Closer Than Expected appeared first on SecurityWeek.
